Narrative_Meaning: Sixes signify harmony and adaptation, but they can also indicate hard work or unexpected challenges.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Horde card signifies a huge crowd and the madness of crowds. Hordes are not calmly queuing citizens. Hordes ravage, pillage and loot, they are rioting, revolutionary, reactionary things. They may be formed from a stampede, panic amongst an orderly crowd can quickly create an unthinking horde.

Significator.Plot: The Horde Plot is about numbers, usually big numbers, occasionally there may be literal mathematics involved, but more normally this is a tale with scores of Characters and plenty of sides. These are Epic plots usually that reflect society from top to bottom, and may span multiple kingdoms or worlds. The main weapon of the Horde Plot is the Sub-Plot, and you can expect additional Arcs and Stories galore, which each Character having at least an Arc of their own, that will interact at least once with one other Character's Story.
Significator.Side: When a side is marked by the Horde, they are legion, they can take Pact-Descendant Embodiments, even if they have none, but most of these Embodiments will be a suitable Archetype Character that meets as many of  the Embodiment criteria as possible.
Significator.Character:
The Horde Significator indicates a Character who is legion. They might be a hive mind, or a linear reincarnation with multi-life recall.

They Gain Yin when they focus on the bigger picture, ignore details, or act without thinking.

They Gain Yang when they react strongly to something they've seen or heard, or share information with others.

They Gain Chi when they work or attack with others.

They gain +1 Success Levels to all actions where they can use their Horde lives together (multiple bodies or information from past lives, etc).
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A figure who is never truly alone, existing as a collective or a recurring soul with memories of many lives. They move with the weight of numbers and the wisdom of the masses, finding strength in unity and possessing a presence that can overwhelm through sheer scale.
Significator.Scene: The Horde Scene is a crowd scene. Crowd scenes are commonly used to invoke a sense of scale, huge crowds have a realism to them all of their own. Yarn-Tellers may play the card to create a large force, usually of allies, to arrive. This often results in an Ordeal, but the Horde can ride in like the Cavalry and lift a bad situation too.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching specifically the Gain of their second Hexagram. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching as their 2nd Hexagram, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The story opens with a crowd scene, perhaps a sporting event, concert, theatre performance or just a busy shopping district. This scene can be Tone setting, with musical leading the way, or brutal sports, or it could be just the setting for a sudden burst of action from a Fray.
Yarn.Gain: Membership (new Pack Descendant or Level of same)
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: Accepted into a larger group or collective, gaining the protection and influence that comes with belonging to a powerful movement or pact.
Yarn.Fray_Description: This Ordeal is usually a high Stakes every one for themselves Motional Ordeal. Stampedes are often caused by another disaster, such as earthquakes, tsunamis, mudslides, floods, fires, and terrorists. The point is huge crowds become very dangerous when everyone starts running in the same direction. Architects design buildings to minimise these risks, but people get both inventive and stupid when the pressure is on.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Numbers Snag can add Numbers to the enemy, multiplying the number of Extras by at least two. This can mean additional forces during the Fray, or it can mean reinforcements afterwards. In some Frays additional Numbers wouldn't hurt, so follow those with a Low to High Stakes Opposed Ordeal against a number of Chorus or Vex Extras.

NPC.Description: The Zombie can be an example of the walking dead, stumbling or running to consume the next thing. Sometimes they are even undead, mostly they are just sleepwalking through life, staring at a phone screen.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs and the pressed side get to party and congregate, letting their hair down together, this can be a bonding moment, or the Opposed Side Embodiment may suffer a Snag while the party is happening.
Yarn.History_Description: The Horde indicates that the current situation was created by a chaotic event, such as a huge riot, enormous melee, peasants revolt, or anarchic revolution.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Horde indicates that there are massed forces around you that are working against authority and probably indirectly you. Revolution is fermenting in the streets and houses, usually this is a revolt of the peasants and middle class against the nobility, slaves against slave-owners, or robot-uprisings against their squishy, juicy masters, but other revolutions are possible.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Horde indicates that the current situation can be resolved most easily by overwhelming it with massive numbers, this might be numbers of people, watts of power, volts of electricity, joules of energy, or even number of tasks, the point is everyone and everything has limits, and can be overwhelmed.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the current situation and plot. It might include information about one side of the Conflict, all the sides and anything in between. It is normal when providing information to always reveal information about the Dominant Side as well as any others.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about how the Embodiment is organized. Are they part of an army, a guild or group, or do they act alone? Are they part of something larger than themselves?
Revelations.Vector_Description: Cries can carry a lot of information, from screams of terror, anguish, pain or despair, through to sobbed words of grieving, yelled insults, and battle orders. Cries are loud, intended to draw attention, although not always to carry information beyond something bad is happening here.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: Who does the Embodiment hang out with? Do they have known allies, friends, a family? The information you get will go some way towards answering these questions.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Public details are information that the general public has access to, if a character is famous for some reason, that reason is public, and the more famous a person is the more public details that will be available about them, from wikipedia entries, magazine articles, recorded interviews and social media posts.

Obstacle.Description: The way ahead may be blocked by a horde, army, rioting crowd, swarm of vermin, or similar danger. The Horde is a powerful Pact of Chorus or Vex Extras, the Pact should be large enough to mimic at least a Minor Guardian.

Tarot.Normal: Target may automatically Stalemate any Test (Scoring exactly the Difficulty). Automatically pay a Stage / Obstacle cost in an Ordeal.
Tarot.Crossed: Adds +1 Success Level, +1 Failure Level and +1 Unsoakable Distract to the target.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: It may be that the way ahead is simply something you have to think about, you may have to strategize. Then again the route may be through a Laboratory, a Library, or similar. Then again maybe the way ahead is bedlam, filled with loud noises, sudden flashes of light, or some other distractions.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: The prisoners! They are escaping on para-wings / hover-boards / giant eagles! Quick, after them!
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Both sides of the battle are now engulfed by confusion. Orders and plans become meaningless, and all Prepared Cards are Discarded. Both sides will take a Failure Level this Stage.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A powerful guardian stands in the way. Usually a large force (dozens) of Chorus Extras, small group of Monster Cast Extras or a Force of Nature Monster with at least 2 ♣ Monster Facets.
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Most herbivorous birds (Parrot) / Rabbit / Hare /  Iguana / Fruit-bat

Stress.Description: Stress and its results can make a person feel lost, directionless and adrift, and usually this leads a Character to wander away from their old life and worries and into new trouble. The thing to remember about trouble is that it can often be quite a lot of fun, at first...
Stress.Rules: Characters who are spiralling are offered a choice. They may take a new Hitch (Lacking, Harrow, Unstable, Defensive, Desires, Doomed) with Banes equal to the the Character’s current Stress, or they may increase another Hitch by 1 Bane for Stress equal to the current Hitch Banes, or they may discard parts of their Character such as Proficiencies, Descendants, Allies or even Annexes which will negate Stress equal to the Cost of the lost part in Chi

Trauma.Description: Trauma can make a Character feel like they are less themselves than before. Such Characters find themselves drawn to share the opinions of others, to do what they do. In extreme cases a Character can experience a complete loss of self, feeling that they have become a gestalt entity with those they are living with, reflecting more the opinons of those around them than any opinion they may have previous had.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma instantly Relieves all Current Stress, but adds a new Persona to the Character’s Personality Annex. Typically this is a "Mocker" Persona, but the Yarn-Teller, Referee, Author or Player can instead pick the Persona of any other Character that the Character spends time with. Additionally, a Deindividualised Character will Relieve 1 Stress whenever they can copy the actions or opinons of another Character, regardless of whether they had other opinions previously.

Age.Description: An Age of Congestion is occurring. During this age a society swells in numbers, but not in territory, leading to overpopulation and complications for the society. This can appear similar to Inundation, however there are no external forces at play only internal pressures, which results in different problems and solutions.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of stifling growth and internal pressure, where the population of society outpaces its borders. It is an era of crowded streets and dwindling resources, where the lack of space breeds tension and forces the culture to look desperately for ways to expand its territory.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Congestion Characters competition for space is greater, this increases Stress for all members of the society based on the difference in the society Group Size versus the Location they occupy as Chi. E.g. a couple of Thousand people (26) require the space of a city block (26) to live comfortably, if they all try to live in a single large public building like a Museum (14) this would create 12 Stress each day for every member of the society. This Stress will often force the society to expand their territory (although this can lead to an Age of Inundation as they encounter exterior forces).
